text: The video shows the placement of trocars for a robotic sigmoid colon resection, with the robotic platform visible in the surgical field.
The video shows the placement of trocars for a robotic sigmoid colon resection, with the robotic platform visible in the surgical setup.

The surgeon begins mobilizing the sigmoid colon using a lateral to medial approach. The descending colon is also mobilized, with the left ureter visualized and the Girota fascia left intact. The sigmoid colon is further mobilized at the promontory from the medial side.

The surgeon begins mobilizing the sigmoid colon using a lateral to medial approach, followed by mobilization of the descending colon. The left ureter is visualized, and the Girota fascia remains intact. The sigmoid colon is then mobilized at the promontory from the medial side.
The surgeon connects the dissection planes and mobilizes the vessels. The vascular pedicle is ligated, followed by the mobilization of the upper part of the rectum.
The surgeon connects the dissection planes and mobilizes the vessels. The vascular pedicle is ligated, followed by mobilization of the upper part of the rectum.

The surgeon transects the bowel using a linear stapler, retracts the specimen through a financial incision, and performs an anastomosis with a circular stapler. A leak test is conducted, showing no leakage, and the surgical wounds are closed. The resected specimen is visible.

The surgeon closes the surgical wounds, and the resected specimen is visible on the surgical field.
